titleStmt.title: Letter from Samuel Shaw to James Shaw, 30 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Samuel Shaw

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Samuel Shaw to his father, James Shaw, reporting that he doesn't not have much news but sends his love to the children and hopes the fishing will improve for his father.
Samuel Henry Shaw (1896-1967) was a sergeant with the 10th Battalion, Royal Inniskilling Fusiliers. He volunteered for active service in September 1914. Before enlisting Samuel was a member of the Portstewart Company, Coleraine Battalion, North Derry Regiment, Ulster Volunteer Force. Samuel served in France, and was wounded during the Battle of the Somme in July 1916 and again in early 1917 and in 1918. Before the War he was employed as a ironmonger's assistant. James Shaw was a local fisherman in the town of Portstewart.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Same Address 30 — 1 — 16 My Dear
Dad Just a few lines
to let you know I am well.
I received your letter and
mothers parcel all right.
There is not much news
as things are just the
usual out here now. I hope
the youngsters are all in
the best of health as this
leaves me. I see by your
letter that the fishing
is still middling. I hope
it will improve. Tell
Mr McAuley I was asking
for him. I think this
is ;all at present. With
love to all. I remain
Your Loving
Son Sammie.
Mr Jas Shaw
Shorncliffe
Portstewart
Co. Derry
Ireland
